name: switch
description: A binary toggle control following the WAI-ARIA APG Switch pattern. Primary variant: <button role="switch" aria-checked> — the native button gives role + Enter/Space + focus for free, no JS. Form variant (asCheckbox=true): <input type="checkbox" role="switch"> for submitting with a form. The thumb slide is driven by CSS attribute selectors (zero JS, CSP-clean). Height-based sm/md/lg sizes (18/22/28px track, --lv-space-5/6/7 thumb) align flush with form controls in a toolbar row. Supports loading state (spinner in thumb, aria-busy), inner on/off labels (aria-hidden, Ant Design style), leading/trailing Content slots for label composition, and the full SAFE wire escaping channel (wireClick + wireArgs + dataAttrs) matching button.jte. The toggle action is wired by the consuming WIRE template; the partial renders the styled snapshot. Net-new tokens: --lv-space-7, --lv-opacity-disabled, --lv-motion-switch-thumb (with prefers-reduced-motion suppression).
docs: Copies the switch partial to your JTE root at lievit/switch.jte, reached as @template.lievit.switch(checked=..., ariaLabel=...). The partial renders a <button role="switch" aria-checked> (primary) or <input type="checkbox" role="switch"> (asCheckbox=true) with a sliding thumb. Size is height-based: sm (18px track), md (22px, default), lg (28px). Wire a toggle SAFELY with wireClick="toggle" plus wireArgs=Map.of("id", row.id()) — same escaping channel as button.jte, no hand-rolled $unsafe needed. Pass leading/trailing (gg.jte.Content) to compose a visible label. Pass ariaLabel or ariaLabelledBy — a switch without an accessible name is an a11y violation. Inner on/off labels (onLabel/offLabel) are aria-hidden; they are decorative, not the accessible name. The source is yours: edit freely.
